text.title: Load Balancing Algorithms Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to apply the lb-algorithms methodology end-to-end.

step.name: measure-traffic-profile
step.input: APM traces or load-test report for the workload behind the LB.
step.action: Compute p50 + p95 + p99 backend response time. Note request mix (short read vs long upload). Note whether autoscaling is in scope and whether clients sit behind NAT/VPN.
step.output: Traffic profile: {p50_ms, p99_ms, request_cost, autoscaling, nat_behind_lb}.
step.decision-gate: If profile is unknown, run a 5-minute synthetic load test before proceeding. Without data, algorithm choice is guesswork.

step.name: check-fleet-capacity
step.action: Mark fleet as homogeneous (all identical) or heterogeneous. For heterogeneous, draft weights proportional to capacity.

step.name: apply-decision-tree
step.action: Walk `content/06-decision-tree.xml`. The tree leads to one of: round-robin, weighted-round-robin, least-connections, weighted-least-connections, least-response-time, cookie-sticky.

step.name: emit-config
step.input: Algorithm + fleet + LB technology (Nginx / HAProxy / cloud LB).
step.action: Render the matching template (`templates/upstream.conf` for Nginx, `templates/backend.cfg` for HAProxy). Insert weights per heterogeneous fleet rule. Record decision JSON to `templates/algorithm-decision.json`.

step.name: validate-and-deploy
step.action: Run `validate-lb-algorithms.py --file algorithm-decision.json`. Commit the LB config + decision JSON to source. Apply to a canary backend pool first; compare p99 before/after.
step.decision-gate: If post-rollout p99 regresses >10%, roll back and re-measure.
